feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Borderlands bros, hopes, fears?
entry.content: Borderlands bros, hopes, fears?

entry.title: /int/ International: Do you have any songs you listen to every day? I start every morning with epitáf…
entry.content: Do you have any songs you listen to every day? I start every morning with epitáfio by titãs, followed by tempo perdido. They remind me to make the most of my time instead of wasting my youth on pointless distractions https://youtube.com/watch?v=7TunQUJ dy1E https://youtube.com/watch?v=qHJ8CIM 4jCw

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: As you walk through the quiet streets of the MAID containment zone, you notice a…
entry.content: As you walk through the quiet streets of the MAID containment zone, you notice a barefoot young man (pic related) leaning against the wall of a derelict building at the town's outer limits. He calls you over and asks if you're looking for a way to take the edge off while you wait for the cleanup crew to arrive. He waves you toward the entrance, asking you to come in with him. What do

entry.content: Guys... it's all about the journey... not about the destination. Y'know, the journey of where literally nothing happens?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I'm gonna give some ratings a reviewer gave some games
entry.content: Tell me if they are legit or not. No, they are not my ratings >Clair obscur - 6.9/10 >Dark souls - 9.1/10 >Ori and the will of the wisps - 9.5/10 >God of war - 6.9/10 >Dishonored 2 - 9.0/10 >Assassin's creed odyssey- 8.1/10 >007 First light - 7.9/10 >Baldur's Gate 3 -7.5/10 >Nine sols - 8.8/10 >Hollow knight - 7.2/10 >Silksong - 6.8/10 >Elden ring - 8.1/10 >Last of us part 2 - 7.0/10 >Sekiro - 7.8/10

entry.title: /g/ Technology: PDF TO AUDIO WEBSITE FEEDBACK
entry.content: Hello , i've made a software (using ai )that converts pdf to audio, no sign up ,no paywall. just upload your pdf and get an audio ,also a downloadable mp3. https://papervoice.onrender.com/ . I wanted to know the user feedback on this. Feel free for constructive criticism

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Invited to meet Peter Jackson, the Tolkien family preferred not to. Why? “They …
entry.content: >Invited to meet Peter Jackson, the Tolkien family preferred not to. Why? “They eviscerated the book by making it an action movie for young people 15 to 25,” Christopher says regretfully. “And it seems that The Hobbit will be the same kind of film.” >The divorce is systematically reactivated by the movies. “Tolkien has become a monster, devoured by his own popularity and absorbed by the absurdity of our time,” Christopher Tolkien observes sadly. “The chasm between the beauty and seriousness of t

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: The Family Channel was a US-based television network active from 1989 to 1997. P…
entry.content: The Family Channel was a US-based television network active from 1989 to 1997. Prior to 1989, it was known under various versions of the name CBN (short for 'Christian Broadcasting Network'). After 1997, it was sold to a consortium jointly owned by Rupert Murdoch and Haim Saban. During its time as The Family Channel, CBN's president Pat Robertson purchased a blimp in order to bring publicity to the network. The blimp was often deployed at NASCAR races and sporting events. The blimp also went on

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Evil Dead Rise
entry.content: After watching the newest Evil Dead, its made me come to appreciate this movie more. I feel its rather underrated, and has a lot of good things going for it. The old apartment complex is a nice setting for a change. Lots of intense moments that still stick with me from watching the first time. A likeable cast of characters. Downsides are lack of kills, and no cameos of Ash.It definitely had better chemistry and gore than the new movie out does.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Reminder that if you say racism on this website you will be more prone to saying…
entry.content: Reminder that if you say racism on this website you will be more prone to saying it in real life as you will get accustomed to saying it. However, once you say it in real life, le consequences will not be just me telling you go back to /pol/. le consequences are you will get jumped. You may also get fired, although I oppose such decisions, as everybody should be employed. Think about this whenever you have le time.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: YT Sux - RSS feeds Plz :)
entry.content: I have been using invidious as a youtube font-end for years now. No front page or shorts present, just a box to search for a video. Had a quick look at the current state of YouTube in 2k26 and oh my god, what a miserable experience. There is so much clutter, distraction and wasted space. Video previews autoplay instantly as you hover over them with an obnoxious colour blob to highlight the current video. Thumbnails are aggressively shocking. It used to be only a few videos which did it but like

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Attempt 5 of learning C++
entry.content: // A number guessing game (Attmept 1 "Copyed from claude") #include // input output #include // for the random function #include // imports time (for the seed) int main(){ // start srand(time(0)); // seeds from time (All random numbers have a seed like how every minecraft world has a seed) int secret = rand() % 100 + 1; // I have NO IDEA what this dose. int secret rendom then ??? +1? int guess = 0; int attempts = 0; // Guesses and attempts std::cout<<"I'm thinking of a number between 1 to 100" <

entry.title: /g/ Technology: when you think about it, Manufacture technology is one of the few industries tha…
entry.content: when you think about it, Manufacture technology is one of the few industries that serves the interests of humanity Engineers keep making technology, especially chips better and better. They're scraping the bottom of the barrel, and doing everything they possibly can to make technology better. And taking science, engineering, and physics to its absolute limits. They have mastered chip manufacture to such a degrees they're up against the limits of what is physically, theoretically possible. Basica

entry.title: /b/ Random: We're roughly at the halfway point of our weekend wank sessions, how's it all go…
entry.content: We're roughly at the halfway point of our weekend wank sessions, how's it all going? I've had a couple of really good ones, a third was ruined when Amazon wanted a fucking signature and another was utterly destroyed when I accidentally wanked out a colossal foul-smelling bottom burp and was entirely unable to continue until the air was fit to breathe again.

entry.title: /b/ Random: How expensive is to live in the us for a year?
entry.content: hello, i will be migrating to idaho either in boise or couer d alane for a couple of years for a job via visa and i am doing a budget of expenses (food, gas for transportation clothing) and i would appreciate if anyone could tell me how much money it takes to live there i dont mean anything fancy like going out to eat every day and expensive clothing, but more day to day expenses any help would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: I have had some experiences which I can only explain as metaphysical, so I belie…
entry.content: I have had some experiences which I can only explain as metaphysical, so I believe in the afterlife. However, I am also I would say a logical person, I don't discredit material events for what they are. Because of this I am keenly aware many of my mind's aspect seem to be chemical and biological, given they can be influenced by even petty elements like the environment or how much water I drank. So it seems to be obvious to me my mind cannot exist as it is in the afterlife. There wouldn't be this

entry.title: /news/ News: Sen. Lindsey Graham Dies Suddenly
entry.content: Thread theme: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dy4 HA3vUv2c https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/po litics-news/sen-lindsey-graham-dies -71-brief-sudden-illness-rcna552722 WASHINGTON — Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who was elected to the Senate in 2003 and was a close political ally of President Donald Trump, has died, his office confirmed early Sunday. He was 71. Graham died Saturday night “from a brief and sudden illness,” his office said in a statement. “Senator Graham’s family appreciates prayers at th

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Surprisingly, during the court battles over the legality of the VCR, the studio…
entry.content: >Surprisingly, during the court battles over the legality of the VCR, the studios began selling their movies on Betamax and VHS—to play on the very devices they were trying to outlaw. Trying to have it both ways, they began selling movies on video cassettes for retail prices up to $100). In 1977, Twentieth Century Fox was the first when they released Patton and MAS*H, which had been in theaters seven years prior, and The Sound of Music, which had its theatrical release in 1965. The first movies
